arts.ttl: Thursday’s briefing: Spurs claim controversial win as West Ham look to new era
arts.sum: Graham Potter is set to be announced as the new Hammers boss on Thursday.

We started the game really well I thought, then we lose Rodrigo (Bentancur, to injury), and in the way it happened, it was quite distressing to see a team-mate go down in that manner. I'm just proud that they feel good tonight, that they get the rewards for their hard work.

arts.ttl: 'I'm 99.9% sure' - Arne Slot shares Stuart Attwell theory after Liverpool anger over decision
arts.sum: Reaction from the Liverpool head coach after the League Cup clash with Tottenham Hotspur

arts.ttl: Tottenham 1-0 Liverpool: Player ratings as late Bergvall goal gives Spurs a Carabao Cup advantage
arts.sum: Liverpool also lost a player in the first half to injury, with Jarell Quansah appearing to pull up with a muscle problem and leaving the pitch after 30 minutes. Both sides had chances to take the lead at various points, with Pedro Porro failing to hit the target at one end after mistake from Liverpool, and Trent Alexander-Arnold seeing an effort cleared off the line at the other.

arts.ttl: Bravery of Tottenham tyros breathes life back into Angeball | Jacob Steinberg
arts.athr: Jacob Steinberg at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium
arts.sum: Archie Gray and Antonin Kinsky lead the way in defeat of Liverpool to give Ange Postecoglou hope for the future A hard-earned, robust, counterpunching 1-0 win against the team who put six past us last month? It’s just who we are, mate. It is of course classic Angeball to kick and hassle opponents into errors, to irritate and snarl in midfield, and to find a ruthless edge just when it seemed that the first leg of this Carabao Cup semi-final was beginning to turn Liverpool’s way following the introduction of Trent Alexander-Arnold, Luis Díaz and Darwin Núñez after an hour of surprising ineptitude from the Premier League leaders. This was a different Tottenham Hotspur from the one that collapsed against Arne Slot’s team 17 days ago. Implausibly, with Cristian Romero and Micky van de Ven still missing in central defence, there was snap and aggression from start to finish. There was Yves Bissouma, who walked a tightrope after an early booking, finding it within himself to dominate Liverpool’s midfield. There was Djed Spence filling in for Destiny Udogie and keeping Mohamed Salah quiet. And there was, perhaps only fleetingly, a sense that life remains in the Ange Postecoglou project, no matter how precarious it has felt for much of a trying campaign. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Liverpool transfer news: Reds eyeing move for out-of-contract Bayern Munich star?
arts.sum: Liverpool are reportedly considering a move to strengthen Arne Slot's squad with the addition of one of Bayern Munich's star players.

arts.ttl: Carabao Cup offers lifeline for Postecoglou and Spurs to cling to
arts.sum: After what has been a challenging month for Ange Postecoglou, the prospect of a cup final may just be focal point that Spurs and their under-pressure boss so desperately need.

arts.ttl: 'Bergvall is headline act as young Spurs show incredible maturity'
arts.sum: Tottenham's trio of young stars give Postecoglou the perfect tonic with Carabao Cup semi-final win against Liverpool.

arts.ttl: 'Obvious mistake' - the Bergvall incident that made Liverpool fume
arts.sum: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk has slammed the referee decision that saw Tottenham match-winner Lucas Bergvall avoid a sending-off for a challenge on Kostas Tsimikas in the Carabao Cup semi-final first leg.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot delivers verdict on controversial Lucas Bergvall decision as Tottenham end Liverpool unbeaten run
arts.sum: Bergvall stopped a Liverpool break when already on a yellow scored and then scored the winner minutes later
